q: The total chemical energy of a substance is called its ___ (or heat content).
___ is the symbol for enthalpy.
The change in enthalpy describes the amount of heat transferred at ___ during a chemical reaction.
Heat changes can be measured by using a ___.
The heat absorbed or released in a process occurring at constant pressure is called the ___.
Enthalpy change is given the symbol ___.
The unit of enthalpy change is ___ ($kJ\ mol^{-1}$).
Enthalpy is not measured directly, however, the change in enthalpy is measured, which is the heat ___ by the system.
For an endothermic reaction where energy is absorbed from the surroundings, the total energy of the products is ___ than that of the reactants.
For an exothermic reaction where energy is released to the surroundings, the total energy of the products is ___ than that of the reactants.
The heat absorbed or released in a process occurring at standard conditions of temperature and pressure is called the ___, $\Delta H^\theta$.
The standard temperature chosen for energy changes is $25\ ^{\circ}C$ = ___ $K$.
The standard pressure chosen for energy changes is $1\ atm$ = $760\ mmHg$ = ___ $kPa$.
A ___ is a balanced chemical equation that includes the physical states of all reactants and products and the standard enthalpy change ($\Delta H^\theta$ is negative or positive).
It is usual to indicate the physical state of each substance by the appropriate letter places in brackets after the formula; ___ for solid.
It is usual to indicate the physical state of each substance by the appropriate letter places in brackets after the formula; ___ for liquid.
The ___ of a reaction ($\Delta H_r^\theta$) is the enthalpy change when the molar quantities of reactants stated in the chemical equation react to give products under standard conditions.
The ___ of a compound is the enthalpy change when one mole of a compound is formed from its elements under standard conditions.
The symbol for standard enthalpy change of formation is ___.
Note that the state symbol for carbon is shown as "___" because it is the most stable form.
The ___ is the enthalpy change when one mole of a substance (element or compound) is burnt completely in oxygen under standard conditions.
The symbol for standard enthalpy change of combustion is ___.
Combustion reactions are always ___ and the enthalpy changes are always negative.
The ___ is the enthalpy change when solutions of an acid and an alkali react together under standard conditions to produce one mole of water.
The symbol for standard enthalpy change of neutralisation is ___.
The neutralisation reaction is exothermic and the enthalpy change is ___.
Experimentally, $\Delta H$ is not measured. The heat change, $q$, is measured by the equation: ___.
In the equation $q = mc\Delta T$, $m$ is the ___ of the reactants.
In the equation $q = mc\Delta T$, $c$ is the ___ of materials.
A ___ is a device to measure the amount of heat released or absorbed in reactions that occur in solutions.
The ___ of a substance, $\Delta H_{sol}$, is the enthalpy change when one mole of the substance is dissolved in water.
Density of water is ___ $g\ cm^{-3}$.
The amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of one gram of water by one degree is known as the ___ of water and is given the symbol $c$.
The specific heat capacity of water has a value of ___ $J\ g^{-1}\ ^{\circ}C^{-1}$.
A ___ is a type of calorimeter for determining the heat of combustion by igniting a sample in a high pressure of oxygen in a sealed vessel and measuring the resulting temperature rise.
